section.heading: Moratoria for species not covered by Convention

paragraph.content: review periodically the trade in species of exotic birds that are not listed in any Appendix to the Convention; and

paragraph.chapeau: after notice and an opportunity for public comment, establish a moratorium or quota on—

subparagraph.chapeau: importation of any species of exotic birds from one or more countries of origin for the species, if the Secretary determines that—

clause.content: the findings described in section 4905(c)(2), (3), and (4) of this title cannot be made with respect to the species; and
the moratorium or quota is necessary for the conservation of the species or is otherwise consistent with the purpose of this chapter; or

subparagraph.chapeau: the importation of all species of exotic birds from a particular country, if—

clause.content: the country has not developed and implemented a management program for exotic birds in trade generally, that ensures both the conservation and the humane treatment of exotic birds during capture, transport, and maintenance; and
the Secretary finds that the moratorium or quota is necessary for the conservation of the species or is otherwise consistent with the purpose of this chapter.

content.p: The Secretary shall terminate a quota or moratorium established under subsection (a) if the Secretary finds that the reasons for establishing the quota or moratorium no longer exist.
